title: quercus ithaburensis subsp. ungeri
intro: Quercus ithaburensis subsp. ungeri is a subspecies of the evergreen tree Quercus ithaburensis, native to the Middle East. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and inflammation, due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Quercus ithaburensis subsp. ungeri include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate gastrointestinal disorders, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its leaves and bark in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and ointments for topical use.
